reasoning: The 30-year TIPS yield (DFII30) ended February at 2.24%, which is the lowest level in the past three months (having fluctuated between 2.25% and 2.59% since late November 2024). Given that it is at a local minimum relative to recent averages, a near-term mean reversion pushes the probability of an increase above 0.50 (estimated at approximately 0.56 for the 1-week horizon). Following the instruction that uncertainty should increase with forecast horizon, the longer-term forecasts uniformly approach 0.50. I discount the FRED seasonal output because absolute yields were simply lower a year ago, rendering the year-over-year threshold test uninformative for daily random-walk directionality.
